title: LEXON-Bench v1.1.0: Executable Obligation Reasoning for AI Regulation
description: Companion artifact for a submission to Artificial Intelligence and Law (Springer). Provides a deterministic synthetic conformance suite (25 clauses, 30 AI system profiles, 750 clause-profile instances) for verifying executable obligation activation, evidence-gap detection, cross-clause conflict detection, and evidence-remediation candidate generation under explicit closed-world assumptions and stratified-Datalog inference. Includes typed obligation, condition, exception, actor, evidence, and system-profile schemas; Datalog-style rule specifications; Python reference implementation; baseline implementations (static checklist, ontology-only, flat-rule engine); illustrative EU AI Act mapping; derivation-trace examples; formal-property tests; evaluation scripts; bootstrap CI computation; paper-ready tables; and a single-command reproduction pipeline. All random seeds are fixed (seed=42); results are deterministic and fully reproducible. This artifact does not provide legal advice, determine legal compliance, or constitute an expert-validated legal corpus.
notes: Companion artifact for an Artificial Intelligence and Law (Springer) submission. This artifact does not provide legal advice, determine legal compliance, or constitute an expert-validated legal corpus. Conformance results: LEXON achieves corpus-level F1=1.000 for T1 (obligation activation) and T2 (evidence-gap detection) on the 150-instance held-out test set; T3 (conflict detection) F1=0.957 (TP=11, FP=1, FN=0). These are conformance-suite results on synthetic data verifying agreement between the reference implementation and the formal conformance oracle. All outputs are reproducible via 'make reproduce' with seed=42. Version history: v1.0.2 archived at DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20399201 (MDPI Information submission artifact). DUAL LICENSE: Source code under MIT License (LICENSE). Synthetic conformance suite data (data/ directory) under CC-BY-4.0 (LICENSE-DATA).
